ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Develops and implements sustainable agriculture practices to minimize environmental impact and maximize crop yields.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Conducts site assessments, identifies areas for improvement, and recommends eco-friendly solutions to farmers, agricultural businesses, and government organizations. Collaborates with stakeholders to create and implement sustainable agriculture plans, ensuring compliance with environmental regulations and industry standards.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: Bachelor's Degree in Agriculture, Environmental Science, or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in agriculture and sustainability can pursue a degree in agriculture or environmental science.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue certifications or bridge courses in agriculture or sustainability.

future_builder.description: Experienced sustainability consultants can advance to senior roles or specialize in specific areas like organic farming or climate change mitigation.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2+ years of experience as a Sustainability Consultant

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into sustainability consulting by acquiring relevant skills and experience.

roadmap.title: Capstone & Portfolio
topics: Develop a Sustainability Plan for a Farm
